caption_text: Table 1 | Differential diagnosis of rotator cuff pathology

Fig. 1 | Structural anatomy of the rotator cuff when viewed from the anterior and posterior aspects.  The tendons of the teres minor, infraspinatus and supraspinatus are continuous on the humeral head. The supraspinatus muscle lies on the superior portion of the scapula and inserts on the anterior aspect of the greater tuberosity of the humeral head. The primary function of the supraspinatus is forward and lateral elevation of the shoulder, and it assists in rotation of the arm. It is the most commonly torn rotator cuff tendon, involved in 70–85% of rotator cuff tears 13 .
